reviews.review_description:
I am really glad I took this game, it's really fun. I didn't get sick, mostly because the sense of height is really good, you can get really scared sometimes (fly to the top of the mountain to see what I mean). Don't let the simple graphics fool you, the flying sensation is great, the game is cool, the missions are challenging, there's quite some action and shooting. 
For such a small price, it's a must buy.
I got my "VR feet" thanks to this game.

reviews.review_title: Flying in vr never felt so free!
reviews.review_description: So my 1st experience in flying in vr was wingsuitvr.  Now that was fun, but omega agent takes it to a whole 'nother level.  You have your own jetpack that you can basically use to fly anywhere in a decent sized city- there are many treasures to be found.  I really enjoy just flying around and exploring the sights.  But you will eventually want to start the missions and see how far you can progress in the game.  The difficulty gets progressively tougher as you progress through the missions, and I have still not beaten all the missions yet, but I've had a great experience with this game.  I hope you will too!

reviews.review_title: Best Gear VR experience so far
reviews.review_description: 60fps.  A great feeling of Immersion.  Challenging and fun game play.   An open world spy-themed jetpack joy ride across an island filled with collectibles and physics based puzzles.  Could really do with a proper campaign mode and some more impressive textures but the visual style of the game is solid and minimalist to make the most of the hardware.  Very happy to see more like this.
All this game is missing is dlc... or a sequel.
Works perfectly on Oculus Go!

reviews.review_title: This game is what VR is for
reviews.review_description: It's the future, and where's my jetpack?  It's inside a wireless VR HMD, and it is glorious.  I got a little sick the first time I played this, as I had with other high-motion games in the past.  Somehow though I kept playing, and it wore off.  I think Omega Agent helped me get my "VR Legs", and now I play any game without a trace of nausea.  I later discovered the "sky dome" setting that serves to further minimize VR sickness, but by then I didn't need it.  The jetpack alone would have been worth the price, it honestly feels incredible to zoom through the oddly empty-but-noisy cartoon spy metropolis and the sewer tunnels underneath and the secret tunnel complex built into the mountain itself (hint: fly into the waterfall).  Now take that, and add a jetpack-mounted shotgun and dogfights with flying saucers.  I'm barely through a third of this game, and it is easily the most fun VR game I've played so far (on the Go).
Too difficult
Kind of pathetic

Im a Vive owner since launch and i alway passed this up for whatever reasone. Got it when i got my gear vr and holy hell this game is great! So far this is my number 1. Could use a little more substance but it is a great experience and really fun to just fly around in. One thing it really needs is support for the xbone s gamepad.

One of my favorites on the Go. Easy to control even with only the Oculus controller. Nice big area to fly around especially when you include all the underground caves. This game tracks a lot of stats and if you do free flight for a hour exploring, you will be ready for the missions. Easy but hard makes it a game for all ages. Super price! Thanks to the makers of a great game.
Installation failed... want my money back
Put it in “Swivel Chair” mode and turning is way better! So fun flying around the city and also searching the island for items. Good game to show people. The kids love it too! :)
This is the first VR experience on Gear VR to feel truly immersive. A must try for those who can handle VR
Awesome
I’m new to The Oculus Go community and after downloading many sub-par games, I decided to buy this game.  I’M VERY PLEASED.  Absolutely satisfied with this game!  Hours of fun.
Could you explain me where are the options as u spoke at the beggining of the game? But not find....:(

reviews.review_title: Absurd inertia and way too unforgiving
reviews.review_description:
I've had my Go for nine months now and never experienced motion sickness or nausea, until now! The controls have an insane amount of inertia, which makes you swing around wildly and really can make you sick. I thought I was immune to VR nausea, but I stand corrected. The inertia needs to be cut in half, to begin with.  This also makes the Jetpack extremely hard to control when you need to manipulate objects to solve a puzzle or hit waypoints etc. in a race.

Which brings me to the second major problem with Omega Agent. How unforgiving and hard it is. It seems that Fireproof Games forgot that games are essentially supposed to be FUN.

Certainly, games can be challenging, but within reason. There is nothing fun about painstakingly trying to manipulate objects into place to open doors, which then close in a matter of seconds in front of you, because you cannot get the inertia-laden Jetpack under control, for example. There is nothing fun about having races with such narrow margins of error that you need to be more or less perfect or you simply fail. The puzzles and races are simply too unforgiving and hard, which takes all the fun out of it and makes it a chore. A frustrating and unrewarding chore.

I'm giving it two stars, because I see the potential in Omega Agent. If the inertia and control problems are addressed and the game is made more forgiving, it could easily be a very fun and entertaining game, indeed. But alas, in its current state, it's not worth more than two stars, which is a shame.

This should be considered for one of the essentials for the Oculus Go! Amazing game and super fun to control the jet pack. Controls are easy and good with the physics. I can spend hours just free roaming in the city which is my favorite feature. Overall amazing and especially for its low price!

This game pulls you in to finding the well hidden items, having you fly through structures, mines, and heights.  Easy to control, with fairly high challenge, while throwing proximity hints as you look all around.  I played this for 20 minutes, until the queasy felling set in.  Good, but in maybe 10 minute intervals.  I will keep it installed.

Very realistic physics... feels like you're flying.  I had motion sickness the first few time I played, but i played through it because it was so fun
  Now, it's all gone away.  Love just chilling a free flying around the city.  Wish there were way more environments like a jungle, alien planet, mines, etc.  Would also be cool to see other players fly around with you... like a multiplayer option
